system.description: Eye lights, another nifty new piece of work, can be either a tool or a weapon. The plain-vanilla light system mounts high-powered, low-heat lights in the cybereye that channel a tight, polarized beam outward down a path parallel to the optical center of the eye. An amount of light sufficient to see with standard low-light eyes falls wherever the user is looking. The tight beam and polarization minimize scatter from the eye, so the light can't be seen unless someone's staring right at you.
